title: hoya insularis
intro: Hoya insularis, also known as the Philippine wax flower, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is native to the Philippines and is known for its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin infections. Hoya insularis is often prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and has been used to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing stress and improving sleep quality.
